title: Rachel Yu
text:
Welcome!
I am a Ph.D. student in the Department of Political Science at the University of California, San Diego. My research interests include Congress, diplomacy, U.S. foreign policy, and U.S.-China relations.
I received a B.A. in Government & Politics and a B.S. in Information Systems from the University of Maryland, College Park in 2021. My research and graduate studies are funded by the NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program and the 21st Century China Center at UCSD.
CV

title: Research
text:
Works in Progress:

Congressional Oversight Overseas: Information Provision through Diplomacy
Messaging at the Water’s Edge: Congressional Polarization and U.S.-China Relations
(with Young Yang)
Foreign Lobbying and Legislative Behavior

Data:

Roberts, Margaret E., Lei Guang, Young Yang, Harris Doshay, and Rachel Yu (2023). Congressional Tweets on China Dashboard.

Relevant Writing:

Yang, Young and Rachel Yu (2023) “Congressional Tweets on China: A Guide to Using the Data Portal”, China Data Lab, 21st Century China Center.

title: Teaching
text:
Teaching Assistance with Sections: Intro to American Politics (Fall 2023), Intro to International Relations (Spring 2024)
Teaching Assistance: U.S. Foreign Policy (Winter 2024), Intro to International Relations (Summer 2022), California Politics (Summer 2022), Voting Rights Act (Winter 2022), Race & Law (Fall 2021), Domestic Politics of China (Spring 2021), Scholarly Writing for the Humanities (Spring 2020-Fall 2021)
Mentorship:

Graduate Advocate for the Summer Training Academy for Research Success (STARS) Program (Summer 2024): Helped organize and facilitate a 7-week research program for undergraduate students from underrepresented backgrounds.
Graduate Tutor for Summer Methods Training (Summer 2023):Organized and facilitated review sessions in methods and topics covered in the core curriculum for UCSD political science Ph.D. students
